title: FIFA Ticket Website Glitch Traps Fans in Wrong Queue — 90-Minute Chaos at Last-Minute Sales Launch
detail: The FIFA last-minute ticket sales phase was marred on April 2 by a significant technical failure. When fans logged on at 11:00 AM ET, FIFA's published links routed buyers into the 'PMA late qualifier supporters sales phase' — a completely separate window reserved for fans of the six newly-qualified teams. Fans sat in the wrong queue for 90+ minutes with no explanation or redirect. FIFA acknowledged around noon that links were 'working properly' but issued no formal apology for the error. The glitch intensified ongoing fan anger over ticket pricing. The FIFA official resale marketplace also reopened on April 2, allowing eligible ticket holders to resell unwanted seats — immediately drawing criticism as a revenue mechanism that legitimizes secondary-market price gouging. US Congress members' letter calling the tournament 'the most financially exclusionary ever' gained renewed attention following the chaos.

title: Pochettino Faces Calls to Rethink Setup After Back-to-Back Pre-Tournament Losses
detail: With 70 days until the World Cup begins, the USMNT's March camp results — a 2–5 loss to Belgium and a 0–2 loss to Portugal — are drawing broad analysis from US soccer media and supporters. Two consecutive defeats, combining for a 2–7 aggregate against opposition ranked 9th (Belgium) and 6th (Portugal) globally, have exposed the team's defensive vulnerabilities under Pochettino's 4-3-3 system. While Portugal were missing key starters Cristiano Ronaldo, Rafael Leão, and Diogo Costa, the results point to systemic issues in defensive transitions and set-piece vulnerability. Pochettino's next opportunity to respond is the May 31 friendly vs Senegal in Charlotte before his May 26 roster announcement. Group D opponents Kosovo, Paraguay, and Australia will all have analyzed these defensive patterns. US Soccer Federation has not indicated any tactical changes.
